title: The fate of the Black Moon
display_title: The fate of the Black Moon
wikitext:
[]: The fate of the Black Moon             Quote Post by henna » Thu Mar 05, 2009 10:55 am When Instrumentality is rejected by Ikari-kun, it looks as if the Black Moon is actually destroyed when the souls (assumedly those who will once more take form) are released from it and Rei/Lilith. I am wondering therefore, what happened to the Black Moon after this. Is it possible/probable that as it is most likely made of Particle Wave Matter, it resumed its location beneath what remains of Tokyo-3, and if so, did it take the structures of NERV with it?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Thu Mar 05, 2009 11:46 am I think it was destroyed somehow after third impact.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Mar 05, 2009 11:54 am It looked like it exploded high above the Earth's surface. Here, it's just bleeding Then it explodes And it's gone

[]:             Quote Post by henna » Thu Mar 05, 2009 12:07 pm See I wonder if it reconstituted elsewhere after it split up. Considering the egg itself seems to be an organism of some kind, it makes one wonder exactly what happened to it.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Mar 05, 2009 12:12 pm Why do you think the Black Moon is an organism. Also, the White Moon was also destroyed during 2I (for different reasons), so it's possible for them to be destroyed.

[]:             Quote Post by henna » Thu Mar 05, 2009 12:19 pm This is true. If the White Moon was destroyed, I suppose the Black Moon could also be destroyed. And I've just realized that the bleeding would have been all the LCL within the Moon itself. A rather shortsighted mistake on my part.

[]:             Quote Post by rberlin » Sat Mar 14, 2009 11:47 pm Where did the black moon come from? I thought it was less a physical object and more a temporary container Rei could materialize to hold everybody's soul. That or it was actually lodged under Geofront, but I thought Geofront was the black moon. Like it was the physical dimensions of the black moon but had no mass until Rei called for it.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Mar 15, 2009 12:32 am It was basically Lilith's Spaceship when she came to this planet. It was never immaterial or needing to be materialized, Giant Naked Rei just pulled it out of the ground.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Sun Mar 15, 2009 6:24 am AuraTwilight wrote: It was never immaterial or needing to be materialized, Giant Naked Rei just pulled it out of the ground.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 15, 2009 8:48 am Your questions are answered in episode 21' -- you do have to read some of the displays shown on screen, to get the full story, but even in the on-air version we have Fuyutsuki: We're getting down very deep. Gendou: Are you worried? Fuyutsuki: Slightly. Fuyutsuki: This is... Gendou: It wasn't us. Someone left this cavity behind, although 89% has been buried. Fuyutsuki: Then, this was originally a spherical underground space. Gendou: That is the installation which is consuming all that humanity has.

I am wondering therefore, what happened to the Black Moon after this. Is it possible/probable that as it is most likely made of Particle Wave Matte
